channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Phishing Campaign Hits 80+ Orgs Using SimpleHelp and ScreenConnect RMM Tools
item.description: An active phishing campaign has been observed targeting multiple vectors since at least April 2025, with legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) software as a way to establish persistent rem...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Backdoored PyTorch Lightning package drops credential stealer
item.description: A malicious version of the PyTorch Lightning package published on the Python Package Index (PyPI) delivers a credential-stealing payload targeting browsers, environment files, and cloud services. [......
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Progress Patches Critical MOVEit Automation Bug Enabling Authentication Bypass
item.description: Progress Software has released updates to address two security flaws in MOVEit Automation, including a critical bug that could result in an authentication bypass. MOVEit Automation (formerly Central) ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Trellix discloses data breach after source code repository hack
item.description: Cybersecurity firm Trellix disclosed a data breach after attackers gained access to "a portion" of its source code repository. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Silver Fox Springs Tax-Themed Attacks on Orgs in India, Russia
item.description: More than 1,600 socially engineered messages from the China-backed advanced persistent threat (APT) group target various sectors to deliver the previously undocumented ABCDoor backdoor, ValleyRAT, and...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: AI-Powered Phishing, Android Spying Tool, Linux Exploit, GitHub RCE & More
item.description: This week, the shadows moved faster than the patches. While most teams were still triaging last month’s alerts, attackers had already turned control panels into kill switches, kernels into open doors,...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: They don’t hack, they borrow: How fraudsters target credit unions
item.description: Fraudsters aren't hacking credit unions, they are exploiting normal business processes. Flare reveals how structured loan fraud methods use stolen identities to pass verification and secure funds. [.....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Progress warns of critical MOVEit Automation auth bypass flaw
item.description: Progress Software warned customers to patch a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in its MOVEit Automation enterprise-grade managed file transfer (MFT) application. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Webinar: Why MSPs must rethink security and backup strategies
item.description: Security breaches don't just test your defenses—they test your recovery. Join Kaseya in our upcoming webinar to learn how MSPs strengthen resilience with SaaS backups and BCDR to stay operational afte...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 2026: The Year of AI-Assisted Attacks
item.description: On December 4, 2025, a 17-year-old was arrested in Osaka under Japan’s Unauthorized Access Prohibition Act. The young man had run malicious code to extract the personal data of over 7 million users of...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Silver Fox Deploys ABCDoor Malware via Tax-Themed Phishing in India and Russia
item.description: The China-based cybercrime group known as Silver Fox has been linked to a new campaign targeting organizations in Russia and India with a new malware called ABCDoor. The activity involved using phishi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA says ‘Copy Fail’ flaw now exploited to root Linux systems
item.description: CISA has warned that threat actors have started exploiting the "Copy Fail" Linux security vulnerability in the wild, one day after Theori researchers disclosed it and shared a proof-of-concept (PoC) e...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: How Dark Reading Lifted Off the Launchpad in 2006
item.description: Twenty years ago, this media brand didn't have a print edition to attract eyeballs and sponsors. Top-notch content and editorial talent did the heavy lifting....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft confirms April Windows updates cause backup failures
item.description: Microsoft has confirmed that the April 2026 security updates are causing failures in third-party backup applications using the psmounterex.sys driver. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Critical cPanel Vulnerability Weaponized to Target Government and MSP Networks
item.description: A previously unknown threat actor has been observed targeting government and military entities in Southeast Asia, alongside a smaller cluster of managed service providers (MSPs) and hosting providers ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Global Crackdown Arrests 276, Shuts 9 Crypto Scam Centers, Seizes $701M
item.description: A coordinated international operation involving U.S. and Chinese authorities has arrested at least 276 suspects and shut down nine scam centers used for cryptocurrency investment fraud schemes targeti...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Instructure confirms data breach, ShinyHunters claims attack
item.description: Educational tech giant Instructure has confirmed that data was stolen in a cyberattack, with the ShinyHunters extortion gang claiming responsibility. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Defender wrongly flags DigiCert certs as Trojan:Win32/Cerdigent.A!dha
item.description: Microsoft Defender is detecting legitimate DigiCert root certificates as Trojan:Win32/Cerdigent.A!dha, resulting in widespread false-positive alerts, and in some cases, removing certificates from Wind...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Telegram Mini Apps abused for crypto scams, Android malware delivery
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered a large-scale fraud operation that uses Telegram's Mini App feature to run crypto scams, impersonate well-known brands, and distribute Android malware. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited Linux Root Access Bug CVE-2026-31431 to KEV
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Friday added a recently disclosed security flaw impacting various Linux distributions to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) c...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Critrical cPanel flaw mass-exploited in "Sorry" ransomware attacks
item.description: A new disclosed cPanel flaw tracked as CVE-2026-41940 is being mass-exploited to breach websites and encrypt data in "Sorry" ransomware attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ConsentFix v3 attacks target Azure with automated OAuth abuse
item.description: A new attack type, dubbed ConsentFix v3, has been circulating on hacker forums, building on the previous technique by adding automation and scaling potential. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Trellix Confirms Source Code Breach With Unauthorized Repository Access
item.description: Cybersecurity company Trellix has announced that it suffered a breach that enabled unauthorized access to a "portion" of its source code. It said it "recently identified" the compromise of its source ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft tests modern Windows Run, says it's faster than legacy dialog
item.description: Microsoft has confirmed that Windows 11 is getting a new modern Run dialog with dark mode support and faster performance in a new preview build. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Edu tech firm Instructure discloses cyber incident, probes impact
item.description: Instructure, the company behind the widely used Canvas learning platform, has disclosed that it recently suffered a cybersecurity incident and is now investigating its impact. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 76% of All Crypto Stolen in 2026 Is Now in North Korea
item.description: North Korean threat actors are pulling off historic cryptocurrency heists on a yearly, sometimes weekly basis now. AI might be helping them....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: 30,000 Facebook Accounts Hacked via Google AppSheet Phishing Campaign
item.description: A newly discovered Vietnamese-linked operation has been observed using a Google AppSheet as a "phishing relay" to distribute phishing emails with an aim to compromise Facebook accounts. The activity h...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 15-year-old detained over French govt agency data breach
item.description: French authorities have detained a 15-year-old suspected of selling data stolen in a cyberattack on France Titres (ANTS), the country's agency for issuing and managing administrative documents. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: If AI's So Smart, Why Does It Keep Deleting Production Databases?
item.description: The issue isn't artificial intelligence, but rather an industry adding AI agent integrations into production environments before proper security testing....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Cybercrime Groups Using Vishing and SSO Abuse in Rapid SaaS Extortion Attacks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are warning of two cybercrime groups that are carrying out "rapid, high-impact attacks" operating almost within the confines of SaaS environments, while leaving minimal trace...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
